problem: Why do amyloid plaques form in Alzheimer's and can they be cleared?
mechanism: Microbiome modulation via targeted bacteriophage therapy
hypothesis: Why do amyloid plaques form in Alzheimer's and can they be cleared? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Microbiome. Microbiome modulation via targeted bacteriophage therapy directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Mouse model knockout study with 31 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: We cannot solve problems with the same thinking that created them. — Einstein paraphrase
